building_name: 335 College St (ASTRO / House of Vapes view)
facade_colour: dark charcoal-black upper facade with white House of Vapes signage and a lower weathered white side building beside a fenced lot

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: dark charcoal-black upper facade with white House of Vapes signage and a lower weathered white side building beside a fenced lot
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: The main dark infill building appears recent and maintained, while the attached low side structure is more worn and graffiti-marked.
photo_observations.window_details: Upper floors use stacked rectangular residential windows in a disciplined modern grid.
photo_observations.door_details: One main glazed storefront entrance fronts the sidewalk, with a secondary door at the low adjoining structure.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Flat modern roofline with simple stepback massing.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Visual impact comes from the stark dark facade and storefront branding rather than ornament.
photo_observations.storefront_description: A narrow modern commercial frontage occupies the main bay, marked by bright House of Vapes signage, full-height glazing, and a secondary entry serving the lower adjoining side structure.
photo_observations.signage_observed: House of Vapes and ASTRO signage are visible.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Recent infill-style upper building beside an older low side volume and adjacent lot conditions.
photo_observations.notes: The most legible night view is IMG_20260321_013410025_HDR.jpg.

facade_description: 335 College St (ASTRO / House of Vapes view) presents a commercial street frontage within the Kensington Market HCD. The street frontage spans roughly 6.1 metres across 3 storeys beneath a flat roof with minor stepback, consistent with its commercial typology. The frontage is defined primarily by mixed in a dark charcoal-black upper facade with white house of vapes signage and a lower weathered white side building beside a fenced lot palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 2, 3, 3, 3, 3 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances. Key storefront and facade features include cornice.
